channel.title: SSbusinessAndMarket
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SSbusinessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: World’s largest oil company reports 25% profit jump as exports via Saudi Arabia’s East-West Pipeline bypass Strait of Hormuz closure
item.description: The pipeline is now operating at its maximum capacity of 7 million barrels of oil per day.

item.title: The global economy is experiencing the largest capex cycle ever, with nearly $5 trillion seen by the end of the decade—and it’s not all AI spending
item.description: At the same time, AI capital spending, while overlapping with the energy transition somewhat, is also galloping ahead full speed.

item.title: Frontier swoops in after Spirit fails while rivals cut capacity
item.description: The airline is adding capacity into airports such as Orlando, Las Vegas and Dallas-Fort Worth, where Spirit had a large presence.

item.title: Netanyahu says war with Iran isn’t over, need to secure uranium
item.description: Netanyahu declined to elaborate when asked if US or Israeli special forces would enter Iran to remove the nuclear material.
